techniques.name: Leak altered documents
techniques.description: Release modified documents as if genuine to support a narrative

techniques.name: Bait legitimate media
techniques.description: Create stories designed to be picked up by legitimate news outlets

techniques.name: Flood the information space
techniques.description: Produce large volumes of content to drown out competing narratives

techniques.name: Use search engine optimization
techniques.description: Manipulate search rankings to promote preferred narratives

techniques.name: Distort facts
techniques.description: Take genuine facts and present them in misleading context

techniques.name: Seed distortions
techniques.description: Introduce small inaccuracies that compound over time

techniques.name: Amplify existing narratives
techniques.description: Boost organic grievances to inflame division

techniques.name: Create fake expert personas
techniques.description: Fabricate authority figures to lend credibility

techniques.name: Use bots or cyborgs
techniques.description: Deploy automated accounts to simulate grassroots support

techniques.name: Organize rallies or events
techniques.description: Create real-world events to validate online narratives

techniques.name: White Noise
techniques.description: Flood the information environment with high-volume, low-signal content to crowd the hypothesis space, making it computationally expensive for audiences and analysts to isolate credible signals from noise; pattern analytically grounded in Briggs et al. 2026 cognitive warfare taxonomy, heuristic pending community empirical validation

techniques.name: Black Noise
techniques.description: Systematically suppress, de-platform, or exclude contradicting sources from the information ecosystem before primary narratives are seeded, shaping the evidentiary baseline so that counter-evidence is absent rather than rebutted; pattern analytically grounded in Briggs et al. 2026 cognitive warfare taxonomy, heuristic pending community empirical validation

techniques.name: Pattern Injection
techniques.description: Seed synchronized narratives that mimic the structural markers of expert consensus, employing fabricated sourcing, credential laundering, false attribution, and mimicry of investigative journalism formats to cause audiences to accept engineered conclusions as independently verified findings; pattern analytically grounded in Briggs et al. 2026 cognitive warfare taxonomy, heuristic pending community empirical validation

common_framing_techniques.name: false_dichotomy
common_framing_techniques.label: False Dichotomy
common_framing_techniques.description: Presenting a complex issue as only two opposing choices, hiding middle ground and shared concerns

common_framing_techniques.name: emotional_amplification
common_framing_techniques.label: Emotional Amplification
common_framing_techniques.description: Using fear, outrage, or disgust to override careful reasoning about a genuinely complex topic

common_framing_techniques.name: scapegoating
common_framing_techniques.label: Scapegoating
common_framing_techniques.description: Blaming a specific group for a systemic problem, redirecting attention from shared structural causes

common_framing_techniques.name: false_equivalence
common_framing_techniques.label: False Equivalence
common_framing_techniques.description: Presenting two very different things as equally valid to create confusion about established evidence

common_framing_techniques.name: cherry_picking
common_framing_techniques.label: Cherry Picking
common_framing_techniques.description: Selecting only evidence that supports one view while ignoring the broader picture

common_framing_techniques.name: appeal_to_fear
common_framing_techniques.label: Appeal to Fear
common_framing_techniques.description: Using threat of harm to prevent rational evaluation of evidence

common_framing_techniques.name: manufactured_consensus
common_framing_techniques.label: Manufactured Consensus
common_framing_techniques.description: Creating the illusion that a fringe view is widely held through coordinated amplification

common_framing_techniques.name: whataboutism
common_framing_techniques.label: Whataboutism
common_framing_techniques.description: Deflecting criticism by pointing to unrelated wrongs, preventing focus on the actual issue

common_framing_techniques.name: none_detected
common_framing_techniques.label: No Manipulation Detected
common_framing_techniques.description: The claim does not appear to use identifiable manipulation techniques
